title: Army of the Potomac - Antietam Campaign, 1862
opening: The army is moving again, and this time the rumor mill agrees on one thing: Lee has crossed the Potomac. The war has come north. Your regiment - hard-used since the Peninsula - draws fresh cartridges and three days' rations, which the veterans say means a fight inside the week. Sergeant Doyle walks the company street at dusk, checking shoes the way other men check rifles. 'Maryland roads are stone,' he says. 'March on your feet now, fight on them later.' Somewhere ahead, beyond South Mountain, forty thousand men in butternut are looking for the same thing you are: a reason this all ends.

squad.name: Sgt. Patrick Doyle
squad.trait: Irish-born, iron patience, keeps a list of every man he's lost

squad.name: Cpl. Nathan Hale Webb
squad.trait: schoolteacher, reads the men's letters aloud

squad.name: Pvt. Charlie Ostrander
squad.trait: sixteen and lied about it, fastest loader in the company

squad.name: Pvt. Gideon Frye
squad.trait: farmer with a brother in the 5th Virginia

squad.name: Levi Freeman
squad.trait: escaped slavery in Virginia; knows every road south, fears recapture more than shells

timelinePressure: 1862-09-14 South Mountain - the passes are forced
1862-09-17 Antietam - the bloodiest single day in American history
1862-09-22 Lincoln issues the preliminary Emancipation Proclamation

title: Army of Northern Virginia - Maryland, 1862
startLocation: Ford on the Potomac near Leesburg, Virginia
opening: The river is cool around your knees and the band on the far bank is playing 'Maryland, My Maryland' badly. The army is barefoot, sunburned, and somehow singing - it has whipped two Union armies this summer and believes General Lee can do anything. Your messmate Eli says Maryland will rise to join the Confederacy; Corporal Settle says Maryland will mostly hide its chickens. Your haversack holds green corn and not much else. Up the column a courier gallops past, raising dust, carrying orders nobody will tell you about.

squad.name: Cpl. Asa Settle
squad.trait: dry wit, third year of war, trusts nothing he can't eat

squad.name: Pvt. Eli Tanner
squad.trait: true believer, writes poetry, terrible shot

squad.name: Pvt. Marcus Boyd
squad.trait: joined to stay beside his cousin, now buries his letters unsent

squad.name: Pvt. John Quill
squad.trait: mountain hunter, reads ground like print

squad.name: Doc Harlan
squad.trait: carries men out under fire and never carries a gun

timelinePressure: 1862-09-13 Union soldiers find Lee's lost Special Order 191 wrapped around three cigars
1862-09-17 Sharpsburg - the army fights for its life along Antietam Creek
1862-09-18 The long retreat across the Potomac
